text:
Heard a stat last week that I can't stop thinking about: at one major dev platform, 80% of new signups are now AI agents. Not humans.

Which means most of us are still building activation flows for the wrong user.

Think about what we ship to new signups: welcome emails, product tours, progress bars, tooltips, the carefully engineered aha moment at minute seven. All of it assumes a human eye, a human attention span, a human deciding whether to come back tomorrow.

An agent doesn't come back tomorrow. It either gets a successful API response in the next two seconds or it moves on and you never see it again.

I spent years optimizing activation funnels. Most of that work would be invisible to an agent. Worse — some of it actively gets in the way (interstitials, email verification, guided setup).

The uncomfortable PM question: if half your new users are machines, what does your activation dashboard actually measure?

What are you tracking that an agent would never trigger?

#AIProduct #ProductManagement #SaaS
topic: AI features in B2B SaaS products

diagram.title: AGENT-FIRST ACTIVATION
diagram.subtitle: How activation changes when your new user is a machine, not a person.

steps.label: DISCOVERABLE
steps.description: Machine-readable docs an agent can parse without a browser.

steps.label: FIRST CALL
steps.description: Successful API response within seconds, no signup required.

steps.label: MEASURABLE OUTPUT
steps.description: Agent can verify the result programmatically and continue its task.

steps.label: USAGE SIGNAL
steps.description: Track calls and outcomes, not sessions or logins.

steps.label: EXPANSION
steps.description: More agents, more calls, priced on consumption not seats.
